# Project Context Instructions

When an AI agent (OpenCode / Claude Code / any coding agent) works on this project,
it should first read the context docs generated by ctx-gen.

## Context Documents Location

- **Main doc (L0+L1+L3)**: `./docs/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md`
- **Per-module details (L2)**: `./docs/modules/<module_id>.md`
- **Raw structured data**: `./.ctx-cache/ctx/*.json`

## How to Use

1. **New agent session?** Read `./docs/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md` first (start with L0 and L1).
2. **Need to edit a specific module?** Read the corresponding `./docs/modules/<id>.md` (L2) before coding.
3. **Context outdated?** Run: `ctx-gen-setup` (re-install) or tell the agent: "update context docs".

## ctx-gen Agent

This project has a `ctx-gen` agent installed (OpenCode only). To use it:
- In OpenCode: switch to the `ctx-gen` agent in the agent panel
- Or say: "use the ctx-gen skill to update context docs"

## Regenerating Context

```bash
# Option 1: Use the ctx-gen agent (OpenCode)
# Just tell it: "regenerate context for this project"

# Option 2: Use the skill directly
# In any OpenCode session: /ctx-gen

# Option 3: Use the MCP tools directly (any MCP-compatible agent)
# scan_skeleton → generate per-module JSONs → validate_coverage → assemble_docs
```

## Note

The `.ctx-cache/` directory is a build artifact. Add to `.gitignore`:
```
.ctx-cache/
docs/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
docs/modules/
```

<!-- END CTX-GEN INSTRUCTIONS -->
